description_long: This is a weathered, vintage-style camper with a boxy, rectangular body and a flat roof featuring two rooftop air conditioning units and a metal roof rack at the rear. The exterior is clad in corrugated metal panels with visible rust and patina, giving it a rugged, well-used appearance. Large, dark-tinted windows line both sides, with several smaller access panels and vents distributed along the lower body. The front features a wide windshield with two side mirrors, while the rear has a ladder for roof access and two square windows. The camper sits on four wheels and has a side entry door, making it suitable for travel and temporary living in urban or suburban environments. The overall design suggests a classic American recreational vehicle, likely constructed primarily from metal and glass, with some plastic and rubber components.
description: A weathered, vintage camper with a boxy metal body, large side windows, rooftop units, and a rear ladder, showing signs of rust and heavy use.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wide windshield, two side mirrors, and the boxy front fascia with rust detailing.
description_view_1: Left side view reveals three large rectangular windows, multiple access panels, and rooftop units.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the roof ladder, two square rear windows, and the metal roof rack.
description_view_3: Right side view highlights a single entry door, several windows, and additional access panels.
attribute_window_configuration: three rectangular windows on the left side, two rectangular windows and one small window on the right side, one large front windshield, two square rear windows
attribute_door_placement: single entry door on the right side near the front
